concerns.text: T010 instructs setting Σ value to `NaN` if not in raw metadata, violating FR-002 which mandates the system MUST compute/extract this descriptor. The task silently relaxes the requirement to 'extract' by allowing a missing state without a fallback computation or explicit halt, weakening the data completeness constraint.

T011 filters for 'ground-truth Σ value (if available)' and proceeds if count >= 500, violating FR-001 (requires records with ALL required features including Σ) and FR-006 (halt if < 500 valid records). This task allows the pipeline to run on a dataset missing a mandatory feature, effectively weakening the 'all required features' constraint.

T016 explicitly instructs to SKIP the MI calculation if ground-truth Σ is missing (Case B), violating FR-007 which mandates the system MUST include non-linear dependency diagnostics and report MI scores. The task creates a silent failure mode where a required diagnostic is omitted, weakening the reporting requirement.

T035 proposes streaming data to handle >7GB datasets, violating Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) and Principle III (Data Hygiene) which require datasets to be checksummed and stored locally (`data/raw/`) to ensure results are reproducible by re-running code against static local data. Streaming bypasses the checksumming and local storage requirement.

T030 defines the R² threshold sweep range as (0.65, 0.70, 0.75), while SC-004 specifies the range as {, 0.70, 0.75} (implied moderate-to-high). Including 0.65 without explicit spec authorization introduces a silent drift in the validation scope, potentially validating against an unapproved threshold.
